call_meta.persona: Parent / family-track learner on government-funded route. Family + Career + Integration motivations. Barriers: Schedule constraints, level-placement failure. Classroom-preferring, which makes her a partial ICP disqualifier for self-paced learning.
call_meta.industry: Not stated (parent at home, family migrant)

jtbd.job: Find a single clear path to learn Dutch from start to finish
jtbd.desired_outcome: A guided A-to-B route she can follow without falling through the cracks between programs
jtbd.current_approach: Piecing together government-subsidized classes, language apps, and previously a mail correspondence course

jtbd.job: Fit Dutch learning around childcare and an unpredictable schedule
jtbd.desired_outcome: Make progress despite young children, pregnancy, and vacation gaps
jtbd.current_approach: Tried evening classes twice; dropped out due to childcare and illness during pregnancy

jtbd.job: Get placed at the correct level
jtbd.desired_outcome: A course that matches where she actually is, not too basic and not mid-stream
jtbd.current_approach: Subsidized class only offered teenager/middelbare-school level; NL training program couldn't place her

jtbd.job: Improve job prospects and family life through Dutch
jtbd.desired_outcome: Access jobs requiring Dutch, connect with her children's friends, handle paperwork more easily
jtbd.current_approach: Getting by in English; relies on extra effort for Dutch paperwork

pains.pain: Scheduling and childcare make fixed-time classes impossible to sustain
pains.trigger: Having children; being pregnant and too sick to attend; vacation periods
pains.cost: Dropped out of classes; little progress over years
pains.verbatim: Had children and became more challenging to find time to go to a class. Tried in the evening twice but it's hard with childcare.

pains.pain: No clear path to learn Dutch
pains.trigger: Fragmented system with separate trajectories (Ukrainians, students, etc.)
pains.cost: Stuck, cannot just follow a route from start to finish
pains.verbatim: No clear path to learn dutch. What can I do to just follow this from A to B.

pains.pain: Level-placement failure -- programs don't know where to put her
pains.trigger: Enrolling in NL training and a subsidized class
pains.cost: Fell through the cracks; offered only a level that didn't fit
pains.verbatim: They did not know where to place me and thought I could jump in the middle.

pains.pain: One-shot government funding raises the stakes on getting it right
pains.trigger: The government-funded class can only be taken once
pains.cost: Afraid to waste the single chance; the class wouldn't start her knowing she'd miss sessions for vacation
pains.verbatim: I'm fine starting this class. It's funded by the government and you can do it only one time. I wanna do it right.

pains.pain: Heavy homework load on top of family responsibilities
pains.trigger: Government programs assign a lot of homework
pains.cost: Harder to keep up alongside childcare
pains.verbatim: These programs have a lot of homework.

pains.trigger: Considered a private tutor as an alternative

workflow_reality.current_process: Relies on government-subsidized classes when one is available at her level; previously used a mail-based correspondence course (booklet plus cassette, returned for correction); has tried language apps and online options when classes don't fit her schedule
workflow_reality.brittleness: Breaks at scheduling (childcare, pregnancy, vacation) and at level placement -- programs can't place her or start her if she'll miss sessions
workarounds: Switching to apps and online when classes don't fit
Leaning on a friend who is training to become a Dutch teacher

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Government-subsidized inburgering-style classes
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Her main route, but blocked by scheduling and wrong-level placement; one-time funding raises the pressure

alternatives_and_competitors.name: NL training program
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Couldn't place her; she fell through the cracks

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Private tutor
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Considered but ruled out on cost

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Language apps
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Tried when classes didn't fit; treated as a fallback, not a path

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Mail correspondence course (booklet + cassette)
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Historical approach -- mailed work back for correction

quotes.text: No clear path to learn dutch. What can I do to just follow this from A to B.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- parent learner
quotes.context: Describing the fragmented system of separate trajectories

quotes.text: They did not know where to place me and thought I could jump in the middle.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- parent learner
quotes.context: On the NL training program failing to place her at the right level

quotes.text: I personally benefit from more an in person class. The interaction with other students... I'm a bit more driven to learn that way.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- parent learner

quotes.text: A lot of job opportunities require dutch. With the friends of my children they don't speak much English... Papers and paperwork in dutch takes more effort.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- parent learner
quotes.context: Answering what speaking Dutch would change in daily life

value_language: follow this from A to B

keyword_candidates.keyword: Dutch course with flexible schedule no fixed class times
keyword_candidates.source_context: Scheduling and childcare conflicts that broke every classroom attempt

keyword_candidates.keyword: how to learn Dutch as a busy parent with young kids
keyword_candidates.source_context: Children, pregnancy, and childcare repeatedly blocking class attendance

keyword_candidates.keyword: clear step by step path to learn Dutch
keyword_candidates.source_context: Wanting a single route from A to B instead of fragmented trajectories

keyword_candidates.keyword: Dutch course placed me at the wrong level
keyword_candidates.source_context: NL training couldn't place her; subsidized class only offered teenager-level content
